name: Western Asturian-Leonese

ancestors.notes: Western Leonese (leonés occidental) as described in Morala & Egido (2009) and the Propuesta de Norma Ortográfica.
Stronger Gallaecian Celtic substrate in the western areas, bordering Galician-speaking territory.
Suebi superstrate, stronger in western Asturias (Suebi kingdom centred in Gallaecia).

notes: Western Asturian-Leonese (Laciana, Alto Sil, Babia, Cabreira, Cepeda). Grounded in Morala & Egido (2009) and the Propuesta de Norma Ortográfica. WESTERN-SPECIFIC FEATURES: (1) DECREASING DIPHTHONGS ⟨ei⟩=/ei̯/, ⟨ou⟩=/ou̯/: *queisu, poucu, cabeiru, roupa, outru, Cabreira* — the most diagnostic marker of western Leonese, absent in eastern varieties (Source A p. 8; Source B §3.2). CONFIDENCE: HIGH. (2) FINAL -u CLOSURE: word-final Latin -O → /u/: *casu, xeitu, territoriu*. -o is legitimate for zones preserving it but -u is the western standard (Source A p. 10; Source B §2.4). Encoded as positional rule: word-final ⟨o⟩ → [u]. CONFIDENCE: HIGH. (3) ⟨gü⟩ + e/i → /gw/: *güerta, güeyu, güelpe* — diéresis obligatory; against institutional ⟨gerra, geyu⟩ (Source B §2.1, §6.3). CONFIDENCE: HIGH. (4) ⟨gu⟩ + e/i → /g/: *guerra* — standard Hispanic convention retained (Source B §2.1). (5) ⟨ch-⟩ = /tʃ/ for PL-/CL-/FL-: *chamar, chover, chegar* — the western standard (Source A p. 16; Source B §3.5). (6) ⟨-e⟩ maintained after /ʃ/: *viaxe, peixe, imaxe* — institutional model stripping /-e/ condemned as non-patrimonial (Source B §4.3; Source A p. 27). CONFIDENCE: HIGH. LACIANA/ALTO SIL SUB-VARIETY ONLY: (7) ⟨l.l⟩ and ⟨ts⟩ = /ts/ (che vaqueira) from Latin L- initial and -LL- geminate only — *l.levar/tsabor* (L-), *botiel.los/vatse* (-LL-). NOT from -LJ- or PL- (Source A p. 9; Source B §3.1). CRITICAL: che vaqueira ≠ /tʃ/; ts/l.l forms must not be merged with ch- forms. (8) -NN- > /n/ (simplified): *cabana, pena, panu* in NW zone (Source A p. 9; Source B §3.6). -KT- > /it/: *teitu, muito* — western standard (Source A p. 10; Source B §3.3). EASTERN DISTINCTION: Eastern Leonese lacks ei/ou diphthongs (monophthongised) and uses -o final.

sources.author: Morala Rodríguez, J.R. & Egido Fernández, M.C.
sources.title: A vueltas con una norma para el leonés
sources.publisher: Instituto Castellano y Leonés de la Lengua, Colección Beltenebros 23
sources.notes: ISBN: 978-84-936383-8-2. Descriptive survey; confirms ei/ou diphthongs as most diagnostic western Leonese marker (p. 8).

sources.author: Anonymous
sources.title: Propuesta de Norma Ortográfica para el Leonés
sources.notes: Prescriptive proposal explicitly using western Leonese as base; mandates gü/gu retention, ei/ou, -u final. Year is approximate (undated document).

sources.author: García Arias, X.Ll.
sources.title: Gramática histórica de la lengua asturiana
